examples.title: Calculate River Discharge (Q = A × V)
examples.description: Algorithm to compute discharge using area-velocity method
examples.explanation: Stream discharge is calculated by dividing river cross-section into segments, measuring velocity at each point, computing area of each segment, and summing Q = Σ(Ai × Vi)
examples.code:
BEGIN
 INPUT number_of_verticals
 total_discharge = 0
 FOR i = 1 TO number_of_verticals
 INPUT depth_at_vertical[i]
 INPUT velocity_at_vertical[i]
 width = measure_width_between_verticals(i)
 area_segment = depth_at_vertical[i] * width
 discharge_segment = area_segment * velocity_at_vertical[i]
 total_discharge = total_discharge + discharge_segment
 END FOR
 OUTPUT total_discharge
END
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n = number of verticals

examples.title: Water Quality Index (WQI) Calculation
examples.description: Simplified algorithm to compute WQI from multiple parameters
examples.explanation: WQI aggregates multiple water quality parameters (pH, DO, BOD, TDS) into a single number (0-100). Each parameter gets a sub-index (si), is weighted (wi), then combined: WQI = Σ(si × wi) / Σwi
examples.code:
BEGIN
 INPUT parameters: pH, DO, BOD, TDS
 // Define sub-index functions (simplified)
 IF pH >= 6.5 AND pH <= 8.5 THEN
 si_pH = 100 - ABS(7 - pH) * 20
 ELSE
 si_pH = 50
 END IF
 
 // DO: higher is better (up to saturation)
 si_DO = min(100, DO * 20)
 
 // BOD: lower is better
 si_BOD = max(0, 100 - BOD * 2)
 
 // TDS: lower is better
 si_TDS = max(0, 100 - (TDS - 100) * 0.1)
 
 // Weights
 w_pH = 1; w_DO = 2; w_BOD = 3; w_TDS = 1
 
 WQI = (si_pH*w_pH + si_DO*w_DO + si_BOD*w_BOD + si_TDS*w_TDS) / (w_pH+w_DO+w_BOD+w_TDS)
 OUTPUT WQI
END

examples.title: Rainwater Harvesting Yield Estimation
examples.description: Calculate potential water collection from rooftop rainwater harvesting system
examples.explanation: Annual water yield = Catchment area × Annual rainfall × Runoff coefficient. Runoff coefficient accounts for losses due to evaporation, spillage, and initial wetting.
examples.code:
BEGIN
 INPUT catchment_area_sqm // Roof area in square meters
 INPUT annual_rainfall_mm // Annual rainfall in millimeters
 INPUT runoff_coefficient // Typical: 0.8 for roofs
 
 // Convert mm to meters
 rainfall_m = annual_rainfall_mm / 1000
 
 // Calculate annual yield
 annual_yield_cubic_m = catchment_area_sqm * rainfall_m * runoff_coefficient
 annual_yield_liters = annual_yield_cubic_m * 1000
 
 OUTPUT "Annual water yield:", annual_yield_cubic_m, "cubic meters"
 OUTPUT "Equivalent to:", annual_yield_liters, "liters"
 
 // Example: 100 sqm roof, 1000mm rainfall, 0.8 coefficient
 // Yield = 100 × 1 × 0.8 = 80 cubic meters = 80,000 liters
END
